title: List UX: new items appear at top after create; paging scrolls back to top
business_reason: Two list usability bugs: (1) after creating a lead the user may not see it (stale page/sort); (2) the pager sits at the bottom, so clicking Next leaves the user scrolled down on a new page.

param_14.question: New items must surface at the top after create; Next/Prev must return the user to the top of the list.
param_14.answer: (1) On create success, reset to page 1 and scroll to top — the list's default sort is already created_at DESC (newest first), so the new row is at the top of page 1. (2) On any page change, scroll the window to top.
param_14.data_entities: school_leads.created_at — list default sort already created_at DESC (actions spec list.order_by); no schema/data change
param_14.downstream_dependencies: ModuleTable is shared (P0.6) — both fixes land for EVERY list page, not just CRM
param_14.assumptions: newest-first is the desired default ordering; 'scroll to top' = window top (sticky toolbar remains visible)

principles_pass.new_at_top: P2.3/P1.x — ordering is server-controlled & already created_at DESC; UI fix = surface it after create (page 1 + scroll)
principles_pass.paging_scroll: UX consistency — page navigation should reorient the user to the start of the new content
